diff_summary: apps/jobs/src/jobs/commission-run.ts (monthly run job: priorMonthPeriod + 3-attempt retry + alert-only failure audit), packages/catalog/src/commission-run.ts (createCommissionRun: claim-by-run_id, monthly unique-period idempotency, priorMonthPeriod UTC boundaries), migration 0061 (commission_runs + partial unique index on monthly period + ledger.run_id), registry/runner wiring. Closes the prior calendar month, sums net unclaimed commission_ledger entries per staff, writes commission_runs + commission_run_lines, audits commission.run.created.
ac_coverage: AC2 (prior-month per-staff totals), AC3 (commission_runs + lines), AC4 (idempotent monthly re-run via partial unique index — tested), AC5 (audit commission.run.created — tested) all implemented & tested. AC1 ("runs at 02:00 on the 1st of each month") is NOT actually implemented: the scheduler fires off intervalMs (30 days) and the cron string is never parsed.

findings.title: AC1 not implemented: scheduler runs on a 30-day setInterval, not the cron "0 2 1 * *" — calendar months drift and can be closed late or processed in the wrong period
findings.detail: The job declares cron "0 2 1 * *" (commission-run.ts:63) but startScheduler (runner.ts:145-164) schedules purely off job.intervalMs via setInterval; the cron string is never parsed (no cron library exists in apps/jobs — confirmed: no cron dep in apps/jobs/package.json, grep finds no node-cron/croner/cron-parser). The job's intervalMs is MONTHLY_MS = 30*24*60*60*1000 (commission-run.ts:7,62). A 30-day fixed interval is NOT a calendar month: it starts from worker boot time and slides ~1 day earlier each month relative to the calendar. Each firing closes priorMonthPeriod(now) (commission-run.ts:106), i.e. the calendar month before the firing INSTANT. Because the firing instant drifts and is decoupled from the 1st, the month a run closes depends on boot time + accumulated drift: a run can fire on the 28th and close the prior month while the current month is still mid-accrual, two firings can land in the same calendar month (the 2nd is an idempotent no-op via the unique index — wasted), and the schedule never self-corrects to 02:00 on the 1st. Worst case across a long-running worker, a firing crosses a calendar boundary such that a given month's close happens days/weeks after month-end (delayed payout) rather than promptly at 02:00 on the 1st as the spec requires. The idempotency guard prevents DOUBLE-paying a month but does NOT guarantee each month is closed exactly once and on time. The job test (commission-run.test.ts:80-89) only asserts job.cron === '0 2 1 * *' and intervalMs > 0 — it never asserts the cron is honored, so the declared-vs-actual mismatch is untested.
findings.fix: Either (a) make startScheduler cron-aware (parse job.cron with a cron-parser and schedule the next 02:00-on-the-1st boundary), or (b) keep the interval but tighten the cadence and make the run self-correcting: run more often than monthly (e.g. daily at 02:00) and have runOnce no-op unless it is the 1st of the month / there is an unclosed prior month — the per-period unique index already makes extra firings safe. Today's 30-day interval is the wrong cadence primitive for a calendar-month close. This needs a product/eng decision on which mechanism to adopt (and whether 02:00 means EAT or UTC — see separate finding), hence decision-needed.

findings.title: Concurrent-race recovery in createCommissionRun queries an ALREADY-ABORTED transaction after the unique-violation catch
findings.detail: createCommissionRun runs entirely inside db.transaction(apply). The monthly-run INSERT (commission-run.ts:101-110, packages/catalog) uses plain .returning() with NO onConflictDoNothing. When a concurrent monthly run for the same period wins the race, this INSERT raises SQLSTATE 23505, which in Postgres aborts the current transaction ("current transaction is aborted, commands ignored until end of transaction block"). The catch block (commission-run.ts:113-127) then runs tx.select(...) and loadLines(tx, ...) on that SAME aborted transaction — those statements will themselves fail, so instead of gracefully returning the existing run with alreadyExisted:true, the function throws an aborted-transaction error. Net effect is masked in practice only because the monthly JOB wraps runOnce in a 3-attempt retry (commission-run.ts:66-79, apps/jobs) and the next attempt's pre-INSERT SELECT (commission-run.ts:64-78, packages/catalog) finds the now-committed competing run and returns the no-op — and the unique index still prevents a double insert — so no double-pay occurs. But the catch path as written is dead/broken and any non-retrying caller (e.g. the ad-hoc route reusing this code path with kind='monthly') would surface a confusing error. Not exercised by tests (PGlite is single-connection, so no concurrent 23505 is ever produced).
findings.fix: Make the INSERT conflict-tolerant instead of relying on a post-abort recovery: add `.onConflictDoNothing({ target: [commissionRuns.periodStart, commissionRuns.periodEnd] })` (matching the partial unique index) and, when `created` is undefined for a monthly run, re-SELECT the existing run (the transaction is still healthy because no error was raised). Alternatively wrap the insert in a SAVEPOINT so the catch's SELECT runs on a non-aborted subtransaction. Either keeps the idempotent no-op working inside one transaction without depending on the outer retry loop.

findings.title: READ COMMITTED race: lines/totals are aggregated from a pre-claim SELECT, so a ledger row committed into the period between the aggregate and the claim is stamped run_id but never paid
findings.detail: createCommissionRun computes `grouped` (per-staff net) over unclaimed in-period entries (commission-run.ts:83-96), then SEPARATELY UPDATEs every unclaimed in-period entry's run_id (commission-run.ts:132-141). Both use the identical predicate (run_id IS NULL AND occurred_at in [start,end)). Under READ COMMITTED, a row committed by a concurrent transaction AFTER the grouped SELECT's snapshot but BEFORE the UPDATE is invisible to the aggregate yet matched-and-claimed by the UPDATE: it gets run_id stamped (so excluded from every future run) but is NOT counted in any commission_run_line — its commission is silently lost. Trigger in this monthly job is narrow because fresh settles carry occurred_at=now() (current month, >= periodEnd) and so don't match the prior-month predicate; but recordBookingCommission/reverseBookingCommission/reassignBookingCommission accept an occurredAt override (commission-hook.ts) and can post a backdated row into the just-closing prior month after the run's aggregate ran. This is the same shared-code defect the P3-E01-S04 reviewer filed as a blocker `patch` (it lives in the shared createCommissionRun). Deferred here to that story's owner to avoid a duplicate/competing fix; flagged because this monthly job is a primary caller of the affected code.
findings.fix: Claim first, then aggregate the rows actually claimed: do `UPDATE commission_ledger SET run_id=... WHERE run_id IS NULL AND occurred_at in [start,end) RETURNING staff_id, amount_cents`, and build the per-staff totals + lines from that RETURNING set — so the lines exactly match what was claimed regardless of concurrent inserts. (Owned by P3-E01-S04's blocker fix.)

findings.title: Period boundary is UTC midnight, not EAT (UTC+3) — bookings in the first 3 hours of the EAT month are paid in the prior month's run
findings.detail: priorMonthPeriod uses getUTCFullYear/getUTCMonth + Date.UTC (commission-run.ts:285-291), so periods are [UTC-midnight-1st, UTC-midnight-1st). The business operates in EAT (UTC+3; the codebase converts to EAT explicitly where it matters, e.g. packages/payments/src/mpesa/stkPush.ts:135-144). A booking settled at e.g. 2026-07-01T01:00 EAT = 2026-06-30T22:00 UTC falls in June's run, and one at 2026-07-01T02:30 EAT = 2026-06-30T23:30 UTC also lands in June — i.e. the last 3 EAT-hours of a month are pushed into the prior month's commission run, and conversely the first 3 EAT-hours of a month are NOT in the EAT-month a payout report claims to cover. This is NOT a contradiction within the system: the staff-facing earnings view (packages/catalog/src/staff-earnings.ts:69-77 monthBoundsUtc) uses the SAME UTC month boundaries, so both views agree on which run an entry lands in, and no entry is double-counted or dropped. It is a money-attribution correctness question (which calendar month a near-midnight booking is paid in) that depends on whether 'month' is defined in EAT or UTC for payouts — a product decision. The spec says 02:00 on the 1st without specifying TZ.
findings.fix: Confirm the intended business definition of a commission month. If EAT: compute the boundary in EAT (shift now by +3h to pick the month, then build periodStart/periodEnd as EAT-midnight expressed as the corresponding UTC instants, e.g. 2026-06-01T00:00 EAT = 2026-05-31T21:00Z), and align staff-earnings monthBoundsUtc the same way so the two stay consistent. If UTC is acceptable, document it explicitly. No code change without that decision.
